text_excerpt: News | Jesse Norman Skip to main content Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE User account menu Log in Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE News Article for the Hereford Times - Hereford in Bloom, and in Spirit 6 November 2025 I am delighted at the wonderful recent news that Hereford has won Gold in the RHS Heart of England in Bloom Awards for 2025. Article for the Hereford Times - National Hospice Week 9 October 2025 This week is National Hospice Week, and it could not come at a more opportune time. Across the country, the UK’s hospice movement is under strain as rarely if ever before. Image Raising awareness of water pollution 22 August 2025 Local MP Jesse Norman is backing a joint effort by Ross Rowing Club and local firm Haigh Environmental to raise awareness of the issue of water pollution in the River Wye, as well as the solutions available to tackle it. Article for the Hereford Times - Northern Ireland veterans 17 July 2025 On Monday, I had the honour of taking part in one of the most extraordinary events of my 15 years in Parliament — a debate on Northern Ireland veterans and the Legacy Act. Article for the Hereford Times - Housing 19 June 2025 As regards housing, there are three numbers you need to know. The first is 82,000: the rough number of houses and flats now in the county. The second is 16,100: the number of new houses scheduled to be built under the last government. The third is 27,260: the number of houses which the present Government wants to be built in Herefordshire over the next 20 years. Article for the Hereford Times - Campus Hereford 22 May 2025 Hereford is entering a quiet revolution, one that has the potential to transform the city for the better and create huge long term potential into the bargain. Article for the Hereford Times - Legacy Act 24 April 2025 Begun by John Major and concluded by Tony Blair with the Good Friday Agreement in 1998, the Northern Irish peace process remains a remarkable achievement. Specialist colleges can save the university system 28 March 2025 This week we have seen scandalous revelations, in The Sunday Times, of fraudulent loan activity at rogue colleges offering courses franchised from established universities. Image Government action on River Wye “just the first step”, says Jesse 11 March 2025 Local MP and long time River Wye campaigner Jesse Norman has given a qualified welcome to news today of the first joined-up action by governments on the river.
